text:
Market today: Mixed and cautious. The real driver for your names is Morgan Stanley flagging chips as resembling the silver trade — a crowded momentum unwind. Mag Seven had one of their worst momentum stretches in 22 years. That's the air coming out of exactly your book.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 16.4%, largest position, on a SELL signal. If it breaks toward that $336 stop, margin amplifies the damage fast. PLTR is down 13.2% and also on SELL — two double-digit losers on margin is real exposure. SPAL is down 16.9% and is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — it's burning even sideways. New, thin, and levered is a dangerous combo right now.

Watch: 1) Whether chip names (AVGO, SMH) stabilize or continue the momentum unwind Morgan Stanley is warning about. 2) SPCX — brand-new IPO, very volatile, almost no price history; the $25B SpaceX bond headline adds complexity.

Bottom line: You have two large losing positions on margin and a decaying leveraged ETF — tighten or cut before the stops make the decision for you.
